objective: Repair Demo Checkpoint 1 acceptance blockers found after merged PR #244 / issue #243. Positive claims: local/dev/test/CI remain mock/local and require no hosted credentials, provider keys, paid services, public URLs, or real provider calls; hosted-demo disclosure metadata cannot echo raw-output, raw-script, provider-payload, media-byte, cloned-identity, or real-person-likeness canaries; encoded unsafe URLs and prompt-injection markers are rejected after bounded canonicalization; idempotent replay cannot bypass invite/session secret checks; session expiry cannot be extended by caller-controlled future timestamps; local/fake deletion and tombstone evidence is server-derived or bound strongly enough that caller-supplied tombstone claims cannot masquerade as authoritative proof; stale active idempotency replay cannot preserve reviewer-visible artifact access after retention/deletion mismatch evidence; repository status and gates identify Checkpoint 1 acceptance repair as the current blocker and cannot misdirect reviewers toward Checkpoint 2 before acceptance passes. Negative invariants: no hosted deployment, public URL exposure, provider account setup, dashboard configuration, paid plan activation, wallet funding, paid spend, production-readiness claim, real provider call, cloned voice, cloned face/avatar, Digital Twin, replica-profile creation, real-person likeness workflow, Product Mode 2, or Checkpoint 2 implementation is authorized.

forbidden: real provider test calls without explicit written owner approval
real avatar or video generation without explicit written owner approval
real TTS generation without explicit written owner approval
